# terrible_awx_awx_schedule (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) Name of this schedule.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `credentials` (String) List of credential names, IDs, or named URLs applied as a prompt, assuming job template prompts for credentials
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) Optional description of this schedule.
- `diff_mode` (Boolean) Enable diff mode for the job template.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Enables processing of this schedule.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `execution_environment` (String) Execution Environment name, ID, or named URL applied as a prompt, assuming job template prompts for execution environment
- `extra_data` (String) Specify `extra_vars` for the template.
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `forks` (Number) Forks applied as a prompt, assuming job template prompts for forks
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `instance_groups` (String) List of Instance Group names, IDs, or named URLs applied as a prompt, assuming job template prompts for instance groups
- `inventory` (String) Inventory name, ID, or named URL applied as a prompt, assuming job template prompts for inventory
- `job_slice_count` (Number) Job Slice Count applied as a prompt, assuming job template prompts for job slice count
- `job_tags` (String) Comma separated list of the tags to use for the job template.
- `job_type` (String) The job type to use for the job template.
- `labels` (String) List of labels applied as a prompt, assuming job template prompts for labels
- `limit` (String) A host pattern to further constrain the list of hosts managed or affected by the playbook
- `new_name` (String) Setting this option will change the existing name (looked up via the name field.
- `organization` (String) The organization name, ID, or named URL the unified job template exists in. Used for looking up the unified job template, not a direct model field.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `rrule` (String) A value representing the schedules iCal recurrence rule. See rrule plugin for help constructing this value
- `scm_branch` (String) Branch to use in job run. Project default used if blank. Only allowed if project allow_override field is set to true.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Desired state of the resource.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `unified_job_template` (String) Name, ID, or named URL of unified job template to schedule. Used to look up an already existing schedule.
- `verbosity` (Number) Control the output level Ansible produces as the playbook runs. 0 - Normal, 1 - Verbose, 2 - More Verbose, 3 - Debug, 4 - Connection Debug.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
